retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Connective Tissue -- Cellular Level -- Collagen Synthesis and Structure
retrieved_docs.content: Procollagen then shuttles to the extracellular space through exocytosis. Once in the extracellular space, it is cleaved at the C-terminus and N-terminus, forming what is now called tropocollagen. As a result of the cleavage, tropocollagen is insoluble in water.

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Connective Tissue -- Cellular Level -- Collagen Synthesis and Structure
retrieved_docs.content: The synthesis of collagen begins with DNA translation of a polypeptide chain in the rough endoplasmic reticulum of intracellular space (See Figure. Review of Collagen Synthesis). This polypeptide chain is called preprocollagen, composed of alpha chains with repeating amino acid glycine-X-Y sequences (X and Y are proline or lysine). [9]

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Connective Tissue -- Cellular Level -- Collagen Synthesis and Structure
retrieved_docs.content: Next, tropocollagen is reinforced with many adjacent tropocollagen molecules by covalent cross-linking of hydroxylated lysine residues, resulting in collagen fibrils. The cross-linking of hydroxylysine residues is made possible through a copper-dependent lysyl oxidase enzyme. Finally, several collagen fibrils accumulate to form a thick bundle; the end product is collagen fibers. [12]

retrieved_docs.title: Anatomy, Tendons -- Structure and Function -- Cell Population and the Extracellular Matrix
retrieved_docs.content: Tenocytes and tenoblasts are specialized fibroblasts that coexist in tendinous tissue. Tenocytes are elongated, while tenoblasts are ovoid. Tenoblasts represent approximately 90% to 95% of tendon fibroblasts. These specialized fibroblasts produce extracellular matrix components, including collagen and proteoglycans. [13] Collagen makes up approximately 65% to 80% of the extracellular matrix. The predominant collagen type in tendons is type I. Type III collagen is present in the epitenon and endotenon. Type II collagen occurs in the osteotendinous junction's fibrocartilaginous areas. Elastin, proteoglycans, and glycoproteins constitute 4%, 4%, and 2% of the extracellular matrix, respectively.

retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Collagen Synthesis -- Fundamentals
retrieved_docs.content: Amino acids are the building blocks of proteins; therefore, it is no surprise that collagen is comprised of amino [4] acids. The primary amino acid sequence of collagen is glycine-proline-X or glycine-X-hydroxyproline [5] X can be any of the other 17 amino acids, and every third amino acid is glycine.

retrieved_docs.title: Enchondroma -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Osteoprogenitor cells: pluripotent mesenchymal stem cells in bone surfaces that growth factors can stimulate to differentiate into osteoblasts Osteoblasts: initiate mineralization and produce, transport, and arrange matrix proteins. Osteoblast actions are regulated by parathyroid hormone, vitamin D, estrogen, growth factors, cytokines, leptin, and low-density lipoprotein-related protein 5. Osteoblasts may become osteocytes or surface-lining cells of bones. Osteocytes: the most numerous cells in bone. Osteocytes participate in key bodily functions like calcium and phosphate homeostasis and cyclic adenosine monophosphate activation. Osteoclast: hematopoietic progenitor cell-derived bone cells responsible for bone resorption Bone matrix proteins include type 1 collagen and noncollagenous osteoblast-derived proteins. retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Collagen Synthesis -- Function
retrieved_docs.content: Collagen is the most abundant protein in the human body. Therefore, it can be divided into many types. The most common types of collagen are types I through V each serving different functions.

retrieved_docs.title: Anatomy, Connective Tissue -- Muscles
retrieved_docs.content: Collagen and elastin compose the dry mass of tendon connective tissue. [8] Tendons are connective tissue structures comprised of a hierarchical arrangement of collagen molecules that arrange into collagen fibrils and then collagen fibers, which can be grouped into primary, secondary, and tertiary bundles. [9] The tendon is surrounded by the epitenon, a fine connective tissue sheath. The complex multidimensional arrangement of the collagen fibers of tendons makes its function possible during the longitudinal, rotational, and transverse forces exerted upon tendons. [9]

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Connective Tissue -- Cellular Level -- Collagen Synthesis and Structure
retrieved_docs.content: Next, the hydroxylated lysine of the alpha chain undergoes glycosylation with the addition of a carbohydrate. After glycosylation, the formation of disulfide bonds and hydrogen bonds between 3 different alpha chains forms a triple helix. The resultant structure is now procollagen. Defective triple helix formation leads to osteogenesis imperfecta and impaired bone matrix synthesis. [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Collagen Synthesis -- Fundamentals
retrieved_docs.content: The process of collagen synthesis occurs mainly in the cells of fibroblasts which are specialized cells with the main function of synthesizing collagen and stroma. Collagen synthesis occurs both intracellularly and extracellularly. Although different types of collagen may undergo different post-translational modifications, the basic outline for collagen synthesis is listed below.

retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Collagen Synthesis -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Collagen can be further divided into several groups depending on the type of structures they form. There are 28 various types of collagen that have been discovered, but by far, the most common are types I through IV, with type I comprising over 90% of the collagen in the human body. [1] [2] [3]

Another group of molecules is glycosaminoglycans (GAG) which attract and retain water, absorb the shock, and impact tissue viscosity. [14] Proteoglycans are a group of proteins in which GAG is attached to one or more core proteins, retaining a similar function to other GAGs. Based on this knowledge, hypotheses about each corneal later can be made. The tear film is a liquid interface with no major polymeric components. Hence it is thought to have little direct mechanical impact on the cornea. The epithelial and endothelial cell layers also lack any regular protein framework; therefore, they contribute little mechanical strength to the cornea. [15]

Deficiency qualifiers are applicable to collagen, immunological adjuvant, estradiol, glucocorticoids, angiotensin amide, sex steroid hormones, sulfur and testosterone. Estradiol is a member of the “Natural and semisynthetic estrogens, plain” group and is used to treat menopausal disorders. Angiotensinamide is a vasoconstrictor that narrows blood vessels and modulates blood‑pressure regulation. Natriuretic peptide type B belongs to the natriuretic substance family, promotes sodium excretion and vasodilation, and counteracts fluid overload. Estrogens are employed for menopausal disorder management, providing hormonal support that alleviates vasomotor symptoms, prevents bone loss and reduces urogenital atrophy. retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 2 -- Community 748
retrieved_docs.content: Hyaluronic acid is classified as a connective‑tissue protein and also functions as an immunological adjuvant; immunostimulants are a subclass of hyaluronic acid. Collagen is likewise a connective‑tissue protein. Both hyaluronic acid and collagen can be described in terms of deficiency and ultrastructure, and the qualifier “deficiency” is applicable to a broad range of entities including hyaluronic acid, collagen, the immunological adjuvant, amylases, asparaginase, estradiol, immunostimulants and prostaglandin antagonists; deficiency may specifically denote a lack of sulfur or testosterone. Amylases belong to the hydrolase enzyme family and may also be qualified by ultrastructure. The ReFit bone substitute is mapped to both hyaluronic acid and collagen, indicating its association with these structural proteins. The drug combination “amylase, phosphates, proteases” is mapped to the enzyme class amylase, indicating inclusion or targeting of amylase activity. Natural and semisynthetic

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 4 -- Community 2784
retrieved_docs.content: Thyroglobulin, blood coagulation factors, and protein hydrolytic enzymes are all entities for which ultrastructural characteristics are recognized as valid descriptors; the ultrastructure of each of these proteins can be examined to elucidate their detailed molecular organization and functional properties.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 3 -- Community 1644
retrieved_docs.content: Follitropin beta is classified within the structural class of gonadotropiny. Gonadotropiny represent a group of reproductive hormones that encompass the lidský choriogonadotropin (human chorionic gonadotropin). This classification indicates that follitropin beta shares key structural characteristics with the human chorionic gonadotropin family, underscoring its role in the regulation of gonadal function and its relevance in therapeutic applications that target the reproductive endocrine system.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 1601
retrieved_docs.content: Estradiol is classified as a hormone, reflecting its role as a key endogenous steroid that regulates reproductive physiology. Gonadotropiny, representing the family of gonadotropins, is a subclass within the broader hormone category, indicating that gonadotropins share fundamental hormonal properties. The group termed “Natural and semisynthetic estrogens, plain” includes estradiol as one of its members, situating estradiol among both naturally occurring and synthetically derived estrogenic compounds used in clinical practice. Choriogonadotropin and follitropin beta are both identified as having the structural class gonadotropiny, meaning they belong to the gonadotropin family and share similar molecular architecture and biological functions related to stimulation of the gonads. Together, these relationships delineate how estradiol fits within the hormone hierarchy, how gonadotropins are organized as a hormonal subclass, and how specific agents such as choriogonadotropin and follitropin beta are structurally related to the gonadotropin class, informing both pharmacologic classification and therapeutic application.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1464
retrieved_docs.content: Choriogonadotropin and follitropin beta are both classified within the gonadotropiny structural class, indicating that they share a common protein architecture characteristic of gonadotropin hormones. As members of this structural class, both agents exhibit similar molecular features that enable them to interact with gonadal receptors and influence reproductive processes. Recognizing that choriogonadotropin and follitropin beta belong to the same gonadotropin family helps clinicians understand their comparable mechanisms of action, potential cross‑reactivity, and appropriate therapeutic uses in assisted reproduction and related endocrine treatments.
